analysis:
1. Bitcoin Downside Risk: Markets Now Pricing Significant Correction Probability

The "Bitcoin dip to $65,000 by December 31, 2026" market has resolved near certainty at 99.9%, surging +42.45pp this week and +18.45pp today alone — the single largest weekly mover in the dataset. The companion "$75,000 dip" market sits identically at 99.9%, up +30.45pp this week. The "$55,000 dip by year-end" market trades at 68% (+35.5pp over the month), and the "$45,000 dip by year-end" market sits at 47% (+26pp over the month). Collectively, the market is pricing a 47% chance Bitcoin revisits $45K before end of 2026 — a structurally bearish long-term posture despite short-term range trading.

2. Bitcoin March Price Levels: Bearish Near-Term Consensus

Bitcoin reaching $150,000 in March is priced at 0.5%, and a new all-time high by March 31 is at 0.5% — both near-zero and the latter down 4.85pp this month. Bitcoin reaching $80,000 in March sits at only 32%, and $90,000 at 3.3%. Conversely, a dip to $65,000 in March prices at 35%, and $60,000 at 14%. The market consensus is that Bitcoin remains trapped in the $70K–$80K corridor for March, with the $75,000 touch market at 71% and $70,000 touch effectively resolved at 99.9%.

3. Token Launch FDV Valuations: Puffpaw, MetaMask, OpenSea Expectations Diverge

Puffpaw's $50M FDV threshold market jumped +18.5pp this week to 62%, while the $100M threshold sits at 34% (+9pp this week) — implying the market expects a modest but not breakout launch. MetaMask above $700M FDV on launch day trades at 34.3% (+4.2pp this week), but has collapsed 39.8pp over the month, signaling sharply faded optimism. OpenSea above $1B FDV sits at 26% (-3pp this week, -36pp this month), with the $500M tier at 54.3% — the market has dramatically repriced OpenSea's token launch expectations downward over recent weeks.

4. MegaETH and Institutional Bitcoin Holdings: Low Expectations, Stable Positioning

MegaETH's $2B FDV launch threshold sits at only 15% (+2pp this week), down 13pp over the month; the $1B tier is at 50% (+1pp this week), down 28pp over the month. The market is pricing a roughly coin-flip on MegaETH clearing $1B FDV but sees a $2B launch as a long shot. On the institutional side, "MicroStrategy sells any Bitcoin in 2025" and "by March 31, 2026" markets sit at 99.9% and 0.8% respectively — the former effectively resolved, the latter unchanged. The "Nothing Ever Happens: MicroStrategy" market at 1.7% rose +5pp this week, suggesting a tiny but growing minority bet on a near-term surprise event.

5. Key Shifts This Week

"Bitcoin dip to $65,000 by December 31, 2026" surged +42.45pp this week to 99.9% — the most dramatic single move in the dataset, reflecting a decisive resolution as current BTC pricing makes this a near-certainty. "Bitcoin dip to $75,000 by December 31, 2026" followed with +30.45pp to 99.9%. Puffpaw $50M FDV jumped +18.5pp to 62%, signaling a late surge of confidence ahead of launch. MetaMask $700M FDV gained +4.2pp this week to 34.3%, a minor recovery against a brutal -39.8pp monthly collapse. "Nothing Ever Happens: MicroStrategy" added +5pp this week to 1.7%, still negligible but its +69pp monthly rise flags sustained speculative interest in a MicroStrategy event before March 31.
